titleStmt.title: Letter from William Ernest Chapman to Robert Chalmers, 30 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William Ernest Chapman

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from William Ernest Chapman (1891-1962), a solicitor, to Robert Chalmers (1858-1938), the assistant under-secretary for Ireland. Chalmers became assistant under-secretary for a brief period following the resignation of Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) following the administrative failure around the 1916 Rising. The letter acknowledges a letter received the previous day. The letter states that Chapman has heard Mary Keegan will return when the doctors allow her to.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5612/9198

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
32, Nicholas Lane,
London E.C. May 30 1916 Dear Sir, I thank you for your
letter of yesterday's date. We have heard from Mary Keegan
that she will return directly the
doctor will allow her to do so and
that the Police have permission to give
her any pass that may be necessary Yours truly Ernest E. Chapman The Under Sec.
Dublin Castle.
